title: machaeranthera scabrella
intro: Machaeranthera scabrella, also known as the Gray Rabbitsfoot, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Machaeranthera scabrella have been found to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting bacterial infections, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Machaeranthera scabrella is often prepared as a tincture or infusion, and can be used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues.
